records.ety: [OE. galle, gal, AS. gealla; akin to D. gal, OS. & OHG. galla, Icel. gall, SW. galla, Dan. galde, L. fel, Gr. <?/, and prob. to E. yellow. √49. See Yellow, and cf. Choler]

defs: The bitter, alkaline, viscid fluid found in the gall bladder, beneath the liver. It consists of the secretion of the liver, or bile, mixed with that of the mucous membrane of the gall bladder.

quotes: He hath . . . compassed me with gall and travail.

records.ety: [F. galle, noix de galle, fr. L. galla.]

defs: An excrescence of any form produced on any part of a plant by insects or their larvae. They are most commonly caused by small Hymenoptera and Diptera which puncture the bark and lay their eggs in the wounds. The larvae live within the galls. Some galls are due to aphids, mites, etc. See Gallnut.
notes: ☞ The galls, or gallnuts, of commerce are produced by insects of the genus Cynips, chiefly on an oak (Quercus infectoria syn. Quercus Lusitanica) of Western Asia and Southern Europe. They contain much tannin, and are used in the manufacture of that article and for making ink and a black dye, as well as in medicine.

defs: To impregnate with a decoction of gallnuts.

records.ety: [OE. gallen; cf. F. galer to scratch, rub, gale scurf, scab, G. galle a disease in horses' feet, an excrescence under the tongue of horses; of uncertain origin. Cf. Gall gallnut.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Galled (gld); p. pr. & vb. n. Galling.]

defs: To fret and wear away by friction; to hurt or break the skin of by rubbing; to chafe; to injure the surface of by attrition; as, a saddle galls the back of a horse; to gall a mast or a cable.
quotes: I am loth to gall a new-healed wound.

defs: To fret; to vex; as, to be galled by sarcasm.
quotes: They that are most galled with my folly, They most must laugh.

defs: To injure; to harass; to annoy; as, the troops were galled by the shot of the enemy.
quotes: In our wars against the French of old, we used to gall them with our longbows, at a greater distance than they could shoot their arrows.

defs: A wound in the skin made by rubbing.
